invariant_definition: A 'great statement' of the Upaniṣads — one of the four canonical Vedāntic Mahāvākyas: *prajñānam brahma* (Aitareya 3.1.3), *aham brahmāsmi* (Bṛhadāraṇyaka 1.4.10), *tat tvam asi* (Chāndogya 6.8.7), *ayam ātmā brahma* (Māṇḍūkya 2). Each is associated with one of the four *Vedas*. Their interpretation is a school-defining matter: Advaita reads them as identity-statements producing *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti*; Viśiṣṭādvaita as *sāmānādhikaraṇya* under *aprthak-siddhi*; Dvaita reanalyzes them (e.g. *atat tvam asi*) or interprets as *sādṛśya*.

per_school.definition: *Mahāvākyas* are identity-statements (*akhaṇḍārtha*); their hearing produces *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti* and removes *avidyā*. The *jahad-ajahallakṣaṇā* construction is required for *tat tvam asi*.
*Mahāvākyas* are *sāmānādhikaraṇya* (co-reference) under *aprthak-siddhi*; *tat* and *tvam* refer to one complex (Brahman-with-jīva-as-body), not to identical referents.
*Tat tvam asi* parsed as *atat tvam asi* (sandhi reanalysis) or read as *sādṛśya*; never as identity.

translator_note: 'Great statement' is the standard gloss. The four Mahāvākyas are technical units; they should be cited untranslated (with gloss) rather than paraphrased.
